tasks.title: Redact query strings in API request logging
evidence: internal/api/util.go loggingMiddleware logs r.URL.RawQuery verbatim, which can include tokens or API keys.
apperrors.RedactString/SanitizeURL exists for redaction but is not used in request logging.
plan: Log a sanitized URL (or redacted query string) instead of raw query parameters.
Use apperrors.RedactString or SanitizeURL consistently for request logs.
Add/update tests to confirm query secrets are redacted in logs.

tasks.title: Respect context cancellation in research orchestration
evidence: internal/research/orchestrate.go continues looping on crawl/scrape errors and never checks ctx.Done inside the URL loop.
Research Run returns a Result even if every target fails, leading to silent success with empty evidence.
plan: Check ctx.Done during the URL loop and return a wrapped ctx error when canceled.
Track success vs failure; return a classified error when all targets fail.
Add tests for cancellation and all-targets-fail scenarios.

tasks.title: Make HTTP fetch retry backoff honor context cancellation
evidence: internal/fetch/http_fetcher.go uses time.Sleep(delay) during retry backoff without checking ctx.Done, so canceled requests still sleep.
Retries continue even if the caller has already canceled the context.
plan: Replace time.Sleep with a select on time.After vs ctx.Done to exit early.
Return a wrapped ctx error when cancellation occurs mid-backoff.
Add tests verifying cancellation stops retries promptly.

tasks.title: Return validation errors for invalid pagination params
evidence: internal/api/util.go parseIntParam silently defaults on parse errors or negatives.
internal/exporter/pagination.go Limit/Offset clamp to defaults instead of returning an error, so invalid inputs never surface to callers.
plan: Introduce strict parsing helpers that return (value, error) for limit/offset.
Update handlers to return apperrors.Validation on invalid pagination params.
Adjust pagination tests to assert 400 responses on invalid input.

tasks.title: Address generated API types file exceeding 1k LOC
evidence: web/src/api/types.gen.ts is 1021 LOC, exceeding the 1,000 LOC threshold requiring split or justification.
Current generator output is a single monolithic types file.
plan: Choose an approach: configure openapi-ts to split types or document a justified exception for generated output.
Update generation config/Makefile and regenerate as needed.
Verify generated files include required file-level docstrings if modified.

tasks.title: Add file-level docstrings to all source and test files
evidence: Multiple source and test files lack top-of-file docstrings describing responsibilities.
Repository policy requires file headers above package declarations.
plan: Add file-level docstrings above package lines for all listed source files.
Add brief headers to all test files.
Run gofmt and biome format after edits.
notes: Consolidated from RQ-0161 through RQ-0172

tasks.title: Modernize TUI with rich Bubble Tea components and real-time progress
evidence: Current TUI is minimal with basic list view only.
No real-time progress indicators for running jobs.
No visual distinction between job states (queued/running/succeeded/failed).
Missing keyboard shortcuts and help view.
plan: Add lipgloss styling with modern color palette and borders.
Implement tabbed navigation: Jobs | Profiles | Schedules | Templates | Crawl States.
Add real-time progress bars for running crawl/research jobs with pages processed counter.
Add detailed job view with expandable sections for logs, errors, and results preview.
Implement vim-style keybindings (j/k navigation, q quit, ? help) with contextual help bar.
Add subtle animations for state transitions and job completions.
notes: Use charmbracelet/lipgloss for styling
Use charmbracelet/bubbles for progress bars and tables
Keep TUI lightweight - no external terminal requirements

tasks.title: Add WebSocket support for real-time job updates in Web UI
evidence: Web UI currently polls for job status updates causing delay and unnecessary requests.
No real-time feedback when jobs start, progress, or complete.
Users must manually refresh to see status changes.
plan: Add WebSocket endpoint /v1/ws for job status streaming.
Implement pub/sub in job manager to broadcast status changes.
Create useWebSocket hook in React for connection management and auto-reconnect.
Update JobList to show live progress indicators and animated status badges.
Add toast notifications for job completions and failures.
Implement connection status indicator (connected/disconnect/reconnecting).
notes: Use gorilla/websocket or native Go websockets
Gracefully degrade to polling if WebSocket unavailable
Add heartbeat/ping-pong for connection health

tasks.title: Design and implement dark mode + polished visual design for Web UI
evidence: Current Web UI uses basic default styling without a cohesive design system.
No dark mode support - expected in modern applications.
Form layouts are dense and overwhelming for new users.
Missing visual hierarchy and consistent spacing scale.
plan: Create CSS custom properties (variables) for light/dark themes.
Implement automatic theme detection from system preferences with manual toggle.
Design and apply consistent spacing scale (4px/8px/16px/24px/32px).
Add smooth transitions and micro-interactions (hover states, focus rings).
Implement card-based layout with subtle shadows and rounded corners.
Add loading skeletons and shimmer effects for better perceived performance.
Ensure WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance (contrast, focus states).
notes: Consider CSS-in-JS or Tailwind for maintainability
Use CSS prefers-color-scheme for system detection
Test on mobile devices for responsive design

tasks.title: Add keyboard shortcuts and command palette to Web UI
evidence: Power users expect keyboard navigation for efficiency.
No quick way to jump between views or actions without mouse.
Command palette pattern is standard in modern apps (VS Code, Linear, etc).
plan: Implement useKeyboard hook for global shortcut handling (Cmd+K, Escape, etc).
Create CommandPalette component with fuzzy search for jobs, actions, and navigation.
Add contextual shortcuts: Ctrl/Cmd+Enter to submit forms, ? for help, / to search.
Show keyboard shortcut hints in tooltips and buttons.
Persist user preferences in localStorage.
notes: Use cmdk or similar for command palette UI
Ensure shortcuts don't conflict with browser/OS defaults
Make shortcuts discoverable with a cheatsheet modal

tasks.title: Implement job templates and quick-start presets in UI
evidence: New users don't know optimal settings for different site types.
Repetitive form filling for common scenarios (SPA scrape, API documentation crawl).
No guidance on when to use headless vs HTTP, Playwright vs Chromedp.
plan: Create preset configurations for common scenarios: Single Page, SPA, Full Site Crawl, API Docs.
Add QuickStartPanel with visual cards for each preset showing estimated time and requirements.
Include smart defaults: auto-enable headless for SPAs, suggest Playwright for React apps.
Add URL-based auto-detection hints (e.g., 'This looks like a Next.js site - enable headless?').
Allow users to save their own presets to localStorage.
notes: Presets should include: name, description, icon, pre-filled form values
Consider popular site patterns: GitHub, Medium, e-commerce, documentation sites

tasks.title: Add visual results explorer with tree view and diff support
evidence: Current results viewer is text-only JSON/JSONL display.
No visual hierarchy for crawl results (hundreds of pages).
Difficult to compare results between job runs.
No visualization of research clusters and citations.
plan: Create hierarchical tree view for crawl results showing site structure.
Add collapsible sections for metadata, links, extracted content.
Implement diff view to compare two job results side-by-side.
Add research-specific visualization: evidence graph, confidence chart, cluster view.
Include export options directly from results view (download CSV, Markdown, JSON).
Add search/filter within results.
notes: Use virtualized lists for large result sets (react-window)
Consider D3 or similar for research visualizations
Add copy-to-clipboard for individual fields

tasks.title: Add onboarding flow and interactive tutorials
evidence: First-time users may be overwhelmed by the many options.
No explanation of extraction templates, pipeline hooks, or render profiles.
Advanced features like auth profiles are hard to discover.
plan: Create step-by-step onboarding for first-time users (can be skipped).
Add interactive tooltips explaining each feature on hover/focus.
Include example walkthroughs: 'Scrape your first page', 'Set up authentication'.
Add inline help text and links to documentation.
Create sample/demo data so users can see results without configuring a real job.
notes: Use react-joyride or similar for guided tours
Track onboarding completion in localStorage
Include 'Playground' mode with mock data

tasks.title: Implement rate limiting dashboard and performance metrics
evidence: No visibility into request rates, success/failure ratios, or performance trends.
Rate limiting is invisible - users don't know when they'll be throttled.
No metrics on crawl speed, extraction accuracy, or resource usage.
plan: Add Prometheus-style metrics endpoint for scraping performance.
Create real-time dashboard showing: requests/sec, success rate, average response time.
Display rate limit status with visual indicator (green/yellow/red).
Add job duration histogram and throughput stats.
Show fetcher usage breakdown (HTTP vs Chromedp vs Playwright).
notes: Keep metrics lightweight - in-memory circular buffers
Don't expose sensitive URLs in metrics
Consider optional Prometheus export endpoint
